questions.q: How many ways are there to choose a $3$-subset from $\{1,2,\ldots,7\}$?
How many non-negative integer solutions does $x_1+x_2+x_3+x_4 = 6$ have?
How many ways are there to arrange the letters of MISSISSIPPI?
questions.explain: The multinomial coefficient $\binom{11}{1,4,4,2}=\frac{11!}{1!\,4!\,4!\,2!}=34650$ is symmetric in the bin sizes, so any ordering of $\{1,4,4,2\}$ gives the same value. The plain $11!$ overcounts because it treats identical letters as distinguishable.

questions.q: Compute the derangement number $D_4$ (permutations of $\{1,2,3,4\}$ with no fixed points).
In a group of $100$ people, $60$ speak French, $50$ speak German, and $20$ speak both. How many speak at least one of the two languages?
How many integers in $\{1,\ldots,100\}$ are divisible by neither $2$ nor $5$?
questions.explain: Divisible by 2: 50; by 5: 20; by both (i.e. by 10): 10. So divisible by 2 or 5 is $50+20-10=60$, and the complement is $100-60=40$.
questions.hint: Count the bad set, subtract from $100$.

questions.q: What is the coefficient of $x^4$ in $(1-x)^{-1} = \sum_{n\ge 0} x^n$?
questions.explain: Every coefficient of the geometric series $\sum x^n$ is $1$.
$\binom{8}{4} = 70$, so $C_4 = 70/5 = 14$. The first Catalan numbers are $1, 1, 2, 5, 14, 42, \ldots$.

questions.q: Which functional equation does the Catalan generating function $C(x) = \sum_{n\ge 0} C_n x^n$ satisfy?
questions.explain: The recurrence $C_{n+1}=\sum_{k=0}^n C_k C_{n-k}$ becomes $C(x) - 1 = x\,C(x)^2$, i.e. $C(x) = 1 + x C(x)^2$. Solving gives $C(x) = (1-\sqrt{1-4x})/(2x)$.
questions.hint: A Dyck path is empty, or starts with an up-step, then a Dyck path, then a down-step, then a Dyck path.

questions.q: How many inversions does the permutation $\sigma = (3, 1, 4, 2)$ in one-line notation have? (An inversion is a pair $i<j$ with $\sigma(i) > \sigma(j)$.)
How many descents does $\sigma = (2, 4, 1, 3, 5)$ have? (A descent is a position $i$ with $\sigma(i) > \sigma(i+1)$.)
What is $\sum_{k=1}^n c(n,k)$ where $c(n,k)$ is the number of permutations of $[n]$ with exactly $k$ cycles?
questions.explain: Every permutation has some number of cycles, so summing over $k$ recovers $|S_n| = n!$. Equivalently, $\sum_k c(n,k) x^k = x(x+1)(x+2)\cdots(x+n-1)$ specialised at $x=1$.

questions.q: Compute $p(5)$, the number of partitions of $5$.

questions.explain: Each part of size $k$ may be used $0, 1, 2, \ldots$ times, contributing the geometric series $1 + q^k + q^{2k} + \cdots = (1-q^k)^{-1}$. The product over $k$ gives Euler's product.
questions.hint: Geometric series for each part size.

questions.q: Euler's identity says: partitions of $n$ into distinct parts are equinumerous with partitions of $n$ into…
questions.explain: The generating-function proof: $\prod_k (1+q^k) = \prod_k \frac{1-q^{2k}}{1-q^k} = \prod_k \frac{1}{1-q^{2k-1}}$ (the even factors cancel). The right-hand side counts partitions into odd parts.

questions.q: How many triangulations does a convex hexagon have? (Use $C_4$.)
questions.explain: Triangulations of a convex $(n+2)$-gon are counted by $C_n$. A hexagon has $n+2 = 6$, so $n = 4$ and $C_4 = 14$.

questions.q: Which family is NOT counted by Catalan numbers $C_n$?
questions.explain: All but the last are Catalan. Set partitions of $[n]$ are counted by the Bell number $B_n$, not the Catalan number. ($B_4 = 15 \ne 14 = C_4$.)
questions.hint: Set partitions are counted by Bell numbers.

questions.q: The RSK correspondence is a bijection between matrices with non-negative integer entries and:
choices: pairs $(P, Q)$ of standard Young tableaux of the same shape
pairs $(P, Q)$ of semistandard Young tableaux of the same shape
questions.explain: RSK in full generality bijects $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$-matrices with pairs of semistandard tableaux of the same shape. Restricted to permutation matrices it gives the Robinson–Schensted bijection between $S_n$ and pairs of standard Young tableaux of the same shape.
questions.hint: Matrices have repeated entries, so the tableaux are semistandard.
